_comment: Scoring source of truth for analyze.py. MUST stay in sync with the forms (build-forms.gs) and the participant docs (../03, ../04, ../05). Concept values are the CORRECT option text per item; matching is normalization-tolerant (curly quotes, en/em dashes, whitespace, case).

A.A1: The detectable difference depends on the ratio of the change to the original size, not the absolute change
A.A2: The smallest change in the stimulus that can just be detected (the JND)
A.A4: The 1% change is far below the just-noticeable difference - the ratio is too small to perceive
A.A5: 10 -> 14, because +4px is a larger fraction of the original size
A.A6: a roughly constant percentage of the current level

B.B1: The detectable difference depends on the ratio of the change to the original weight, not the absolute change
B.B2: A constant fraction that stays roughly the same for a given sense
B.B4: The change is below the brightness JND (about 8%), so the state change cannot be perceived
B.B5: 10 -> 12, because it is a larger fraction of the original count
B.B6: a roughly constant ratio (percentage) between consecutive sizes

A.A4: Initial state, goal state, and operators
A.A5: A good representation exposes useful sub-goals and shrinks the search space
A.A6: operators - the available actions that move you from one state to another

B.B4: Starting state, target state, and the available actions (operators)
B.B5: Changing the representation can turn a hard problem into an easy one
B.B6: operators - the available actions you can take

A.A3: STM holds about 7 +/- 2 items briefly; LTM stores knowledge effectively permanently
A.A5: Each labelled group becomes a single chunk, lowering the number of items STM must track

B.B3: STM holds a few items briefly; LTM stores knowledge permanently and with vast capacity
B.B4: group them into about 5 labelled sections
B.B5: Break it into a few grouped stages with progress indicators, so each stage is one chunk
